source_input.brief: Bold, sophisticated home with dramatic moody interiors.

pinterest_cluster_labels: dark minimalist bedroom — black walls + vertical wood slats
moody luxe bedside styling — brass lamps & layered bedding
Japandi noir living room — smoked oak slats, shoji screens & bonsai

pins.title: Dark Bedroom with Wooden Touches.
pins.description: Warm, moody tones meet natural wood for a cozy and elegant bedroom.  #DarkBedroom #WoodDetails #CozyHome
pins.cluster_label: dark minimalist bedroom — black walls + vertical wood slats

pins.title: Moody Black Luxe Bedroom — Dark, Cozy & Ultra-Chic 🖤🔥
pins.description: Bold black tones and gold accents elevate this modern moody bedroom into a luxury retreat. Save this dramatic and elegant look for your dream room inspiration! #moodybedroom #blackbedroomdecor #modernluxestyle
pins.cluster_label: moody luxe bedside styling — brass lamps & layered bedding

pins.title: Japandi Noir Living Room Ideas: Dark Minimal Decor with Black Tadelakt & Smoked Oak (2025 Trend)
pins.description: Discover Japandi Noir: a serene, moody living room with matte black tadelakt walls, smoked oak slats, a charcoal linen sectional, and a monolithic black stone coffee table. Warm gray microcement floors, frosted shoji screens, a single bonsai, and soft skylight glow deliver calm, textural minimalism. Save for dark interior design ideas, modern wabi-sabi styling, and Scandinavian–Japanese living room inspiration.
pins.cluster_label: Japandi noir living room — smoked oak slats, shoji screens & bonsai

cluster_summary.label: dark minimalist bedroom — black walls + vertical wood slats
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Matte black walls and ceiling, vertical walnut/smoked oak slat accent, low wood platform bed and matching nightstands, charcoal linens, thin pendant lights and warm cove LED. Stage with a single small plant, minimal bedside objects, and expansive glazing to balance heaviness.

cluster_summary.label: moody luxe bedside styling — brass lamps & layered bedding
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Upholstered dark headboard, layered neutrals (light gray sheets + charcoal duvet), sculptural brass dome bedside lamps, floating shelves with curated ceramics/art, recessed LED strip behind ribbed panel. Stage with textured throws and 1–2 sculptural metallic accents for warmth.

cluster_summary.label: Japandi noir living room — smoked oak slats, shoji screens & bonsai
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Matte black tadelakt or painted walls, smoked oak vertical slats, low charcoal linen sectional, monolithic black marble coffee table, frosted shoji screens and a single bonsai for organic focus. Stage with a neutral woven rug, low clutter, and tactile cushions to emphasize calm minimalism.

vibe_report.summary: A restrained, textural Japandi‑inspired interior that balances deep, matte dark surfaces with warm natural wood and minimal, sculptural accents. The mood is calm and intimate: low ambient illumination, focused warm accent lights, and deliberately curated negative space. Materials read tactile and honest — smoked oak, matte black stone, warm brass — with a limited warm‑neutral palette to keep visual cohesion across connected spaces.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Use an anchored dark base (#0B0B0B matte black and #2F2F2F charcoal) with smoked oak warmth (#73503A) and restrained warm accents (gold/amber accent ~#D99A48) plus mid‑neutral grays (#8F8F8F). This keeps contrast dramatic but avoids cold, saturated hues; warm tones prevent the scheme from feeling austere.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Predominantly soft ambient with strong directional window pulls where available. Layered practicals (warm 2200–2700K LED strips, brass task lamps, low‑glow pendants) provide intimate pools of light. Shadows should be crisp and sculptural but never starkly underexposed — preserve readable midtones.
materials: matte painted plaster (deep black) for primary walls
honed black marble / matte black stone for coffee and kitchen surfaces
warm wool and natural fiber rugs
furniture_language: low, grounded silhouettes (platform beds, low sofas)
clean horizontal planes, minimal visible legs
sleek, thin metal accents in warm brass
styling_rules: limit palette to 3 dominant neutrals + 1 warm accent; repeat materials across visible rooms
curate 3–5 surface objects per view maximum (sculptural/ceramic/plant)
use single living focal plant (bonsai) rather than multiple houseplants
keep textiles monochrome or subtly varied neutrals — no patterns
expose vertical slats on at least one primary sightline to create depth and shadow
avoid: high‑gloss reflective metals in large areas
excess clutter (paper stacks, many small chargers, toys)

room_guidance.headline: Japandi Noir Living — Calm, Sculptural Centerpiece
room_guidance.guidance: Center the composition on the low charcoal sofa facing the large north window. Anchor with a large neutral woven rug and the monolithic black marble coffee table. Show the smoked oak vertical slat feature on the adjacent wall with a warm recessed strip behind it. Add a single sculptural bonsai as the organic counterpoint. Lighting: rely on natural bay/window light for soft directional pull; supplement with a warm brass floor lamp and recessed cove at the slat wall for depth.
must_include: low-profile 3-seater sofa — charcoal slate linen upholstery (#2F2F2F), low recessed plinth base in smoked walnut finish, single continuous back cushion, no visible legs from side angle
large neutral woven rug — warm pebble wool, rectangular to fit sofa and coffee table
round low coffee table — honed black marble (Jet Black #0B0B0B) with beveled edge, solid cylindrical base
smoked oak vertical slat divider — floor-to-ceiling smoked oak veneer, vertical 2-inch slats, matte finish
single medium bonsai in shallow matte black ceramic pot
must_avoid: visible patterned cushions or colorful throws
bright surface clutter on coffee table

room_guidance.headline: Compact Japandi Kitchen — Minimal, Textured, Functional
room_guidance.guidance: Keep cabinetry simple, matte charcoal or warm stone, with a narrow matte black stone counter and subtle brass pulls. Use the small east window to create a soft directional highlight over the sink. Because the kitchen opens to the living area, allow sightlines to include the sofa, rug and coffee table; proportions must keep the island/counter small to avoid visual crowding. Lighting: a slim linear brass pendant over the island/small peninsula (warm 2200K) and undercabinet warm task lighting.
must_include: linear brass pendant — matte brass finish, slim rectangular profile, warm 2200K light, suspended 30 inches above island
matte black stone kitchen counter — honed black finish (consistent with coffee table material)

sightline-visible living objects: low-profile 3-seater sofa — charcoal slate linen upholstery (#2F2F2F), large neutral woven rug — warm pebble wool, round low coffee table — honed black marble (Jet Black #0B0B0B) with beveled edge, solid cylindrical base
must_avoid: oversized island that blocks view to living
open shelving cluttered with mismatched dishware

room_guidance.headline: Primary Bedroom — Dark Luxe, Textural Bed Foil
room_guidance.guidance: Create a low wood platform bed with layered charcoal and warm gray linens. Use a smoked oak or ribbed wood headboard against a matte black accent wall and a pair of small sculptural brass sconces or pendant lamps. Keep bedside surfaces minimal — one ceramic object and a folded neutral throw. Lighting: west/south window provides soft late‑day light; ambient recessed cove behind headboard for warm halo.
must_include: low wood platform bed in smoked oak with charcoal linen headboard
layered bedding: light gray sheets, charcoal duvet, warm gray textured throw
pair of small brass bedside sconces (warm 2700K)
must_avoid: high beds or bulky tall furniture that contradicts low-profile language
bright bedside lamps or cold white lighting

room_guidance.headline: Secondary Bedroom / Work Nook — Calm and Functional
room_guidance.guidance: This room functions as a medium bedroom with a modest standing desk placed at a window side to benefit from daylight. Keep palette consistent: matte black accent wall with smoked oak shelving and a small upholstered bed or daybed. Styling with a single narrow bookshelf for curated objects.
must_include: compact standing desk — dark smoked oak top, matte black steel legs (placed near window)
narrow open bookshelf — smoked oak shelves, minimal vertical metal frame
low-profile bed/daybed in charcoal linen
must_avoid: large office chairs that dominate the room

room_guidance.headline: Guest / Small Bedroom — Minimal and Quiet
room_guidance.guidance: Small footprint — emphasize breathing room. Use a low bed, minimal bedside shelf, and one small sculptural lamp. Keep visual weight low near the doorway to preserve circulation.
must_include: low bed platform with charcoal linens
single sculptural bedside lamp in matte brass
must_avoid: oversized wardrobes that reduce perceived space

warnings: Kitchen is compact — avoid suggesting a full-size island or large dining table in camera compositions.
Open sightlines mean shared materials/furniture must remain visually consistent between kitchen and living; ensure color/finish specs are reused exactly.

creative_spec.overall_mood: intimate, cinematic, quietly luxurious — dark tonal anchors warmed by smoked oak and brass accents
global_style_notes: Preserve architecture and sightlines exactly; use furniture to reinforce horizontal planes.
Use directional window light as primary driver for editorial shadows; practicals should supplement rather than compete.
Keep props to a minimum — one ceramic, one book, one small plant per vignette.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: blur, distort, low quality, warped architecture, flicker, unstable camera, unrealistic lighting, muddy shadows, layout drift, no color inconsistency between rooms, no mismatched furniture across sightlines

caption_context.property_brief: Bold, sophisticated home with dramatic moody interiors.
caption_context.aesthetic_summary: A restrained, textural Japandi‑inspired interior that balances deep, matte dark surfaces with warm natural wood and minimal, sculptural accents. The mood is calm and intimate: low ambient illumination, focused warm accent lights, and deliberately curated negative space. Materials read tactile and honest — smoked oak, matte black stone, warm brass — with a limited warm‑neutral palette to keep visual cohesion across connected spaces.

annotations.label: Mood
annotations.value: intimate, cinematic, quietly luxurious — dark tonal anchors warmed by smoked oak and brass accents

warnings.message: Maintain strict color/finish reuse for shared_objects across sightlines to avoid visible mismatches.
Kitchen footprint is small — any proposed island or furniture must be dimensionally modest (peninsula depth <= 36") to preserve circulation and sightlines.

For video generation follow AutoHDR flow: perform 1 image refinement pass for rooms with flat lighting (kitchen and bedrooms) before video; living room may be direct-to-video if a strong edited photo exists.
Negative prompt enforcement required: no color inconsistency between rooms, no mismatched furniture across sightlines; also block warped architecture and unstable camera motion.
Kitchen is compact — avoid suggesting a full-size island or large dining table in camera compositions.
Open sightlines mean shared materials/furniture must remain visually consistent between kitchen and living; ensure color/finish specs are reused exactly.
